commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Rent, as people mourning. Literally, "dirty. "Hebrew and Septuagint, "cut. "(Haydock) (Chap. xvi. 6., and Leviticus xix. 28.)
Lord, at Masphath, where people performed their devotions, as well as they could, Judges xx. 1., and 1 Machabees iii. 46.) Jeremias had probably ordered an altar to be erected, as Samuel had done, 1 Kings vii. 6. (Calmet)
Others think that these people went thither for a priest, to offer what they had brought on the altar of Jerusalem, Baruch i. 10. (Tirinus)
Masphath lay to the south. (Calmet)
It seems many of the people about Samaria were true believers, and even more affected at the destruction of the temple. (Haydock)
Their city had been demolished by Salmanasar (Calmet) long before, and many fresh inhabitants introduced, who adhered to idols. (Haydock)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
